author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXLV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …The title is, “Praise, to David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Praise to Christ Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since He is called David, who came to us of the seed of David, yet He was our King, ruling us, and bringing us into His kingdom, therefore “Praise to David himself” is understood to mean, Praise to Christ Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ according to the flesh is David, because He is the Son of David: but according to His Divine Nature He is the Creator of David, and Lord of David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will exalt Thee, my God, my King; and I will bless Thy Name for the age, and age upon age” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see that the praise of God is here begun, and this praise is carried on even to the end of the Psalm.…Now then begin to praise, if thou intendest to praise for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who will not praise in this transitory “age,” will be silent when “age upon age” has come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But lest any one should in any otherwise also understand what he saith, “I will praise Thy Name for the age,” and should seek another age, wherein to praise, he saith, “Every day will I bless Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Praise then and bless the Lord thy God every day, that when single days have passed, and there has come one day without end, thou mayest go from praise to praise, as “from strength to strength.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No day shall pass by, wherein I bless Thee not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it is no wonder, if in thy day of joy thou bless the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if perchance some day of sorrow hath dawned on thee, as is natural in the circumstances of our mortal nature, as there is abundance of offences, as temptations are multiplied; what, if something sad befall thee, a man; wilt thou cease to praise God? wilt thou cease to bless thy Creator?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou cease, thou hast lied in saying, “every day,” etc. But if thou cease not, although it seem to thee to be ill with thee in the day of thy sorrow, yet in thy God it shall be well with thee.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Great is the Lord, and very much to be praised” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much was he about to say? what terms was he about to seek?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How vast a conception hath he included in the one word, “very much”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Imagine what thou wilt, for how can that be imagined, which cannot be contained?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He is very much to be praised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And of His Greatness there is no end;” therefore said he “very much:” lest perchance thou begin to wish to praise, and think that thou canst reach the end of His praises, whose Greatness can have no end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not then that He, whose Greatness has no end, can ever be enough praised by thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it not then better that as He has no end, so neither should thy praise have end?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His Greatness is without end; let thy praise also be without end.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how great things besides has His boundless Goodness and illimitable Greatness made, which we do not know!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When we lift the gaze of our eyes even to the heaven, and then recall it from sun, moon, and stars to the earth, and there is all this space where our sight can wander; beyond the heavens who can extend the eyesight of his mind, not to say of his flesh?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So far then as His works are known to us, let us praise Him through His works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Generation and generation shall praise Thy works” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every generation shall praise Thy works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For perhaps every generation is meant by “generation and generation.”…Did he perchance mean to imply two generations by that repetition?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we are in this generation sons of God, we shall be in another generation sons of the Resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Scripture hath called us “sons of the Resurrection;” the Resurrection itself it hath called Regeneration.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the regeneration,” it saith, “when the Son of Man shall be seated in His Majesty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also in another place; “For they shall not marry, nor be given in marriage, for they are the sons of the Resurrection.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “generation and generation shall praise Thy works.…And they shall tell out Thine excellence.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For neither shall they praise Thy works, save in order to “tell out Thine excellence.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Boys at school are set to praise, and all such things are set before them to be praised, as God hath wrought: a mortal is set to praise the sun, the sky, the earth; to come to even lesser things, to praise a rose, or a laurel; all these are works of God: they are set, they are undertaken, they are praised: the works are lauded, of the Worker they are silent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I desire in the works to praise the Creator: I love not a thankless praiser.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou praise what He hath made, and art silent of Him who made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that which thou seest, what is it that thou praisest?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The form, the usefulness, some virtue, some power in the things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If beauty delight thee, what is more beautiful than the Maker?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If usefulness be praised, what more useful than He who made all things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If excellence be praised, what more excellent than He by whom all things were made?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall speak of the magnificence of the glory of Thy Holiness, and shall record Thy wondrous deeds” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the excellence of Thy fearful works shall they speak of: and Thy greatness, they shall relate it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The remembrance of the abundance of Thy sweetness they shall pour forth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7): none but Thine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See whether this man, meditating on Thy works, hath turned aside from the Worker to the work: see whether he hath sunk from Him who made, to the things which He made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of the things which He hath made, he hath made a step up to Him, not a descent from Him to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou love these more than Him, thou wilt not have Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what profit is it to thee to overflow with the works, if the Worker leave thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly thou shouldest love them; but love Him more, and love them for His sake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He doth not hold out promises, without holding out threats also: if He held out no promises, there would be no encouragement; if He held out no threats, there would be no correction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that praise Thee therefore shall “speak” also “of the excellence of Thy terrible deeds;” the excellence of that work of Thy hands which punisheth and administereth discipline, they shall speak of, they shall not be silent: for they shall not proclaim Thine everlasting kingdom, and be silent about Thine everlasting fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the praise of God, setting thee in the way, ought to show thee both what thou shouldest love, and what thou shouldest fear; what thou shouldest seek, and what thou shouldest shun; what thou shouldest choose, and what thou shouldest avoid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The time of choice is now, the time of receiving will be hereafter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then the excellence of Thy terrible things be told.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unlimited as it is, though “of Thy greatness there is no end,” they shall not be silent about it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How shall they recount it, if there is no end of it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They shall recount it when they praise it; and because there is no end of it, so of His praise also there shall be no end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The remembrance of the abundance of Thy sweetness they shall pour forth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O happy feasts!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall they eat, who thus shall “pour forth”!…So eat, that thou mayest pour forth again; so receive, that thou mayest give.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou eatest, when thou learnest; thou pourest forth again, when thou teachest: thou eatest, when thou hearest; thou pourest forth again, when thou preachest; but that thou pourest forth, which thou hast first eaten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Finally, that most eager feaster John, to whom the very table of the Lord sufficed not, unless he leaned on the Lord’s breast, and of his inmost heart drank in divine secrets; what did he pour forth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is it that it sufficeth not to say, “Thy remembrance;” or, “the remembrance of Thine abundance”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, what availeth it if it be abundant, yet not sweet?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also it is annoying if it be sweet but too little.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6. …By “pouring forth” this, His preachers “shall exult in His righteousness” not in their own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then hast Thou done unto us, O Lord, whom we praise, that we should be, that we should praise, that we should “exult in Thy righteousness,” that we should “utter forth the remembrance of the abundance of Thy sweetness”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us tell it, and, as we tell, let us praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Merciful and pitiful is the Lord; long-suffering, and very merciful” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sweet is the Lord to all, and His compassions reach into all His works” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were He not such as this, there would be no seeking to recover us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider thyself: what didst thou deserve, O sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Despiser of God, what didst thou deserve?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See if aught occur to thee but penalty, if aught occur to thee but punishment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou seest then what was due to thee, and what He hath given, who gave gratis.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was given pardon to the sinner; there was given the spirit of justification; there was given charity and love, wherein thou mayest do all good works; and beyond this, He will give thee also life everlasting, and fellowship with the angels: all of His mercy.…Hear the Scripture: “I will not the death of a sinner, but rather that he should turn, and live.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By these words of God, he is brought back to hope; but there is another snare to be feared, lest through this very hope he sin the more.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then didst thou also say, thou who through hope sinnest yet more?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whensoever I turn, God will forgive me all; I will do whatsoever I will.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say not then, “To-morrow I will turn, to-morrow I will please God; and all to-day’s and yesterday’s deeds shall be forgiven me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou sayest true: God hath promised pardon to thy conversion; He hath not promised a to-morrow to thy delay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sweet is the Lord to all, and His compassions are over all His works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then doth He condemn? why doth He scourge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are not they whom He condemneth, whom He scourgeth, His works?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Plainly they are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And wilt thou know how “His compassions are over all His works”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence is that long-suffering, whereby “He maketh His sun to rise on the evil and on the good.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are not “His compassions over all His works, who sendeth rain upon the just and upon the unjust”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In His long-suffering He waiteth for the sinner, saying, “Turn ye to Me, and I will turn to you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are not “His compassions over all His works”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when He saith, “Go ye into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels,” this is not His compassion, but His severity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His compassion is given to His works: His severity is not over His works, but over thy works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, if thou remove thine own evil works, and there remain in thee nought but His work, His compassion will not leave thee: but if thou leavest not thy works, there will be severity over thy works, not over His works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let all Thy works, O Lord, confess to Thee, and let Thy saints bless Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not the earth His work?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are not the trees His work?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Cattle, beasts, fish, fowl, are not they His works?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Plainly they too are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how shall these too confess to Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I see indeed in the angels that His works confess to Him, for the angels are His works: and men are His works; and when men confess to Him, His works confess to Him; but have trees and stones the voice of confession?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yes, verily; “let all” His “works confess to” Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sayest thou? even the earth and the trees?…But there ariseth the same question in regard of praise, as in regard of confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if earth and all things devoid of sensation therefore cannot confess, because they have no voice to confess with; neither will they be able to praise, because they have no voice to proclaim with.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But do not those Three Children enumerate all things, as they walked amid the harmless flames, who had leisure not only not to fear, but even to praise God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They say to all things, heavenly and earthly, “Bless ye the Lord, praise Him and magnify Him for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold how they praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none think that the dumb stone or dumb animal hath reason wherewith to comprehend God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who have thought this, have erred far from the truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God hath ordered everything, and made everything: to some He hath given sense and understanding and immortality, as to the angels; to some He hath given sense and understanding with mortality, as to man; to some He hath given bodily sense, yet gave them not understanding, or immortality, as to cattle: to some He hath given neither sense, nor understanding, nor immortality, as to herbs, trees, stones: yet even these cannot be wanting in their kind, and by certain degrees He hath ordered His creation, from earth up to heaven, from visible to invisible, from mortal to immortal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This framework of creation, this most perfectly ordered beauty, ascending from lowest to highest, descending from highest to lowest, never broken, but tempered together of things unlike, all praiseth God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore then doth all praise God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because when thou considerest it, and seest its beauty, thou in it praisest God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The beauty of the earth is a kind of voice of the dumb earth.…And this which thou hast found in it, is the very voice of its confession, that thou praise the Creator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou hast thought on the universal beauty of this world, doth not its very beauty as it were with one voice answer thee, “I made not myself, God made me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when Thy saints bless Thee, what say they?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall tell the glory of Thy kingdom, and talk of Thy Power” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How powerful is God, who hath made the earth! how powerful is God, who hath filled the earth with good things! how powerful is God, who hath given to the animals each its own life! how powerful is God, who hath given different seeds to the womb of the earth, that they might make to spring up such various shoots, such beautiful trees! how powerful, how great is God!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou ask, creation answereth, and by its answer, as by the confession of the creature, thou, O saint of God, blessest God, and “talkest of His power.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That they may make known to the sons of men Thy power, and the glory of the greatness of the beauty of Thy kingdom” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy saints then commend “the glory of the greatness of the beauty of Thy kingdom,” the glory of the greatness of its beauty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a certain “greatness of the beauty of Thy kingdom:” that is, Thy kingdom hath beauty, and great beauty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since whatever hath beauty, hath beauty from Thee, how great beauty hath Thy whole kingdom!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not the kingdom frighten us: it hath beauty also, wherewith to delight us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is that beauty, which the saints shall hereafter enjoy, to whom it shall be said, “Come, ye blessed of My Father, enjoy the kingdom”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence shall they come? whither shall they come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, brethren, and, if ye can, as far as ye can, think of the beauty of that kingdom which is to come; whence our prayer saith, “Thy kingdom come.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that kingdom we desire may come, that kingdom the saints proclaim to be coming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe this world: it is beautiful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How beautiful are earth, sea, air, heavens, stars.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not all these frighten him who considereth them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not the beauty of them so conspicuous, that it seemeth as though nothing more beautiful could be found?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And here, in this beauty, in this fairness almost unspeakable, here worm and mice and all creeping things of the earth live with thee, they live with thee in all this beauty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great is the beauty of that kingdom where none but angels live with Thee!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a greatness of a certain beauty; let it be loved before it is seen, that when it is seen, it may be retained.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy kingdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What kingdom mean I? “a kingdom of all ages.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the kingdom of this age too hath its own beauty, but there is not in it that greatness of beauty, such as in the “kingdom of all ages.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thy dominion is in every generation and generation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the repetition we noticed, signifying either every generation, or the generation which will be after this generation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Faithful is the Lord in His words, and holy in all His works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Faithful is the Lord in His words:” for what hath He promised that He hath not given?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Faithful is the Lord in His words.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hereto there are certain things which He hath promised, and hath not given; but let Him be believed from the things which He hath given.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We might well believe Him, if He only spake: He willed not that we should believe Him speaking, but that we should have His Scriptures in our hands:…as though a kind of bond of God’s, which all who pass by might read, and might keep to the path of its promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how great things hath He already paid in accordance with that bond!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do men hesitate to believe Him concerning the Resurrection of the dead and the Life to come, which alone now remaineth to be paid, when, if He come to reckon with the unbelievers, the unbelievers must blush?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If God say to thee, “Thou hast My bond: I have promised judgment, the separation of good and bad, everlasting life for the faithful, and wilt thou not believe?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There in My bond read all that I have promised, reckon with me: verily even by counting up what I have paid, thou canst believe that I shall pay what still I owe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that bond thou hast My only-begotten Son promised, “Whom I spared not, but gave Him up for you all:” reckon this then among what is paid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Read the bond: I promised therein that I would give by My Son the earnest of the Holy Spirit: reckon that as paid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I promised therein the blood and the crowns of the glorious Martyrs; let the White Mass remind you that My debt has been paid.…He setteth before the eyes of all His payment of His debts: some He hath paid in the time of our ancestors, which we saw not: some He hath paid in our times, which they saw not; throughout all generations He hath paid what was written.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what remaineth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do men not believe Him, when He hath paid all this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What remaineth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold thou hast reckoned: all this He hath paid: is He become unfaithful for the few things which remain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “the Lord is faithful in His words, and holy in all His works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord strengtheneth all that are falling” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who are “all that are falling”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All indeed fall in a general sense, but he meaneth those who fall in a particular way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many fall from Him, many also fall from their own imaginations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they had evil imaginations, they fall from them, and “God strengthened all that are falling.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who lose anything in this world, yet are holy, are as it were dishonoured in this world, from rich become poor, from honoured of low estate, yet are they God’s saints; they are, as it were, falling.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “God strengtheneth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “the just falleth seven times, and riseth again; but the wicked shall be weakened in evils.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When evils befall the wicked, they are weakened thereby; when evils befall the righteous, “the Lord strengtheneth all that are falling.”…“And lifteth up all those that have been cast down:” all, that is, who belong to him; for “God resisteth the proud.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The eyes of all hope upon Thee, and Thou givest them food in due season” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as when thou refreshest a sick man in due season, when he ought to receive, then Thou givest, and what he ought to receive, that Thou givest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes then men long, and he giveth not: he who tendeth, knoweth the time to give.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore say I this, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest any one be faint, if perchance he hath not been heard, when making some righteous request of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he maketh any unrighteous request, he is heard to his punishment: but when making some righteous request of God, if perchance he have not been heard, let him not be down-hearted, let him not faint, let his eyes wait for the food, which He giveth in due season.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When He giveth not, He therefore giveth not, lest that which He giveth do harm.…“Thou givest them meat in due season.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou openest Thine Hand, and fillest every living thing with blessing” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though sometimes Thou givest not, yet “in due season” Thou givest: Thou delayest, not deniest, and that in due season.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Righteous is the Lord in all His ways, and holy in all His works” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both when He smiteth and when He healeth, He is righteous, and in Him unrighteousness is not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Finally, all His saints, when set in the midst of tribulation, have first praised His righteousness, and so sought His blessings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They first have said, “What Thou doest is righteous.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So did Daniel ask, and other holy men: “Righteous are Thy judgments: rightly have we suffered: deservedly have we suffered.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They laid not unrighteousness to God, they laid not to Him injustice and folly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First they praised Him scourging, and so they felt Him feeding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is nigh unto all that call upon Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where then is that, “Then shall they call upon Me, and I will not hear them”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See then what follows: “all who call upon Him in truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many call upon Him, but not in truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They seek something else from Him, but seek not Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why lovest thou God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because He hath made me whole.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is clear: it was He that made thee so.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from none else cometh health, save Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because He gave me,” saith another, “a rich wife, whereas I before had nothing, and one that obeyeth me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This too He gave: thou sayest true.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He gave me,” saith another, “sons many and good, He gave me a household, He gave me all good things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou love Him for this?…Therefore if God is good, who hath given thee what thou hast, how much more blessed wilt thou be when He hath given thee Himself!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast desired all these things of Him: I beseech thee desire of Him Himself also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these things are not truly sweeter than He is, nor in any way are they to be compared to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then who preferreth God Himself to all the things which he has received, whereat he rejoiceth, to the things he has received, he “calleth upon God in truth.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He will perform the will of them that fear Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will perform it, He will perform it: though He perform it not at once, yet He will perform it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly if therefore thou fearest God, that thou mayest do His will, behold even He in a manner ministereth to thee; He doeth thy will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He shall hear their prayer, and save them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou seest that for this purpose the Physician hears, that He may save.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle telling thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For we are saved in hope: but hope which is seen is not hope: but if what we see not we hope for, then do we with patience wait for it: “the salvation,” that is, which Peter calleth “ready to be revealed in the last time.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord guardeth all that love Him, and all sinners He will destroy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou seest that there is severity with Him, with whom is so great sweetness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will save all that hope in Him, all the faithful, all that fear Him, all that call upon Him in truth: “and all sinners He will destroy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “all sinners,” save those who persevere in sin; who dare to blame God, not themselves; who daily argue against God; who despair of pardon for their sins, and from this very despair heap up their sins; or who perversely promise themselves pardon, and through this very promise depart not from their sins and impiety?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The time will come for all these to be separated, and for the two divisions to be made of them, one on the right hand, the other on the left; and for the righteous to receive the everlasting Kingdom, the wicked to go into everlasting fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since this is so, and we have heard the blessing of the Lord, the works of the Lord, the wondrous things of the Lord, the mercies of the Lord, the severity of the Lord, His Providence over all His works, the confession of all His works; observe how He concludeth in His praise, “My mouth shall speak the praise of the Lord, and let all flesh bless His holy Name for ever and ever” (ver.
